structured.title: Family Books Summer Camping Trip
structured.overview: The user and their partner discuss final touches on a written appendix that needs to be uploaded (not printed or bound), then focus on planning a family camping holiday. They compare campsite options, tent sizes, and how awnings or gazebos affect pitch choice, recalling a previous positive experience at Stowford. They agree to go ahead and book a specific campsite, acknowledging it may be a bit exposed in windy weather but generally looks good for the kids to run around and play football. They settle on a Friday‑to‑Friday booking to minimize use of annual leave, aligning it with the children’s school schedule (finishing on the 17th and fitting around the user’s graduation). They note that taking that specific week off will make their overall summer absence from work seem shorter and discuss that August will still be busy, so only shorter breaks will be possible then. They briefly acknowledge potential Friday traffic but plan to leave early, and the conversation ends with the user being encouraged and congratulated on their progress and decisions.

action_items.description: Book the campsite for the family trip

apps_results.content:
## Appendix and submission  
- Appendix added, extends document pages 10–50+  
- Work will be uploaded, not printed or bound  

## Campsite choice and tent size  
- Considering specific campsite with large pitches  
- Large pitch fits 7m tent; extra-large up to 8.3m  
- Tent plus awning/trailer should still fit  

## Campsite features and comparison  
- Site looks decent but possibly windy/exposed  
- Comparing to previous favorite campsite Stowford  
- Need space for boys to run and play football  

## Booking details and timing  
- Decision made to book the campsite  
- Likely Friday–Friday stay to use 4 days leave  
- Aware of potential Friday traffic; plan to leave early  

## Holiday scheduling around work and school  
- Kids finish school on 17th; graduation then  
- Holiday in second week of break; 3 weeks after remain  
- August is busy at work; can take short breaks, not full week  

## Next steps  
- Finalize campsite booking and deposit  
- Confirm exact dates with work and school calendar  
- Plan packing and early departure to avoid traffic
